name: Congee - Rice Gruel
recipeIngredient: 1 Piece tangerine peel, soaked to soften and minced
recipeInstructions:
In all my years of eating and cooking Chinese food, I'd never tried Congee (rice gruel) before yesterday. It just seemed so bland that I thought it couldn't possibly be all that interesting. I was WRONG! The recipe below gives a subtle but hearty "soup" which can be garnished in a virtually infinite number of ways according to personal taste. I found that I like just the basic stuff, unembellished the best.
1. Substitute half a cup of long grain rice and half a cup of glutinous rice for the long grain rice.
2. Combine rice, stock, preserved turnip, ginger, and tangerine peel in a large soup pot.
3. Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer uncovered for approximately 1-1 1/2 hours or until the rice is thoroughly broken up.
4. Stir occasionally to prevent the soup from sticking and add boiling water if necessary.
5. When done, the soup should be thick and creamy. Add salt to taste.
6. Garnish with any of the suggested garnishes: chopped green onion, chopped coriander, slivered preserved ginger, and sliced tea melon.
Variations:
Just before serving, add cooked chicken, pork, ham, or beef. Or with rice, add diced forest mushrooms (soaked to soften) or dried shrimp.
